Your working directory is: /work/output/artifacts/skill-bpmn-e2e-invoice-exception-triage

Create a new local Maestro BPMN Process Orchestration project named
InvoiceExceptionTriage. The project must be fully synthetic and public-safe.

Model this process:
- Manual start event accepts invoiceId and amount variables.
- Script task classifyInvoice sets a risk value.
- Exclusive gateway routeByRisk sends low-risk invoices to an auto-approved
  end event and high-risk invoices to a user task named Review invoice.
- The human review path ends at a reviewed end event.

Include BPMN DI shapes/edges for all visible elements and create the package
metadata files expected by the skill:
project.uiproj, bindings_v2.json, entry-points.json, operate.json, and
package-descriptor.json.

Do NOT upload, publish, deploy, debug, run a process, ask for approval,
confirmation, or feedback. Do NOT pause between planning and implementation.
Build the complete local project end-to-end in a single pass.

commands.result_summary: Launching skill: uipath:uipath-maestro-bpmn

1	# Structural BPMN (what the registry does not emit)
2	
3	The registry's `xmlTemplate`s give you the `uipath:*` payload for each node
4	(see [registry-workflow.md](registry-workflow.md)). They do **not** give you the
5	structural BPMN that holds those nodes together. This file is the ground truth
6	for everything you author by hand around the templates.
7	
8	Two sources define the contract:
9	
10	- the registry spec `bpmn-spec.json` — enumerates which BPMN element types and
11	  event definitions exist, via its `bpmnElements` section;
12	- the Studio Web canvas serializer
13	  (`PO.Frontend/src/services/serialization/`) — defines how that XML must be
14	  shaped to import and round-trip.
15	
16	Where the registry stops, the canvas serializer is authoritative. Each
17	gap below is labelled **REGISTRY GAP** — the registry exposes no template for
18	it, so author it from this reference.
19	
20	## The document scaffold (REGISTRY GAP)
21	
22	The registry emits no `<bpmn:definitions>` / `<bpmn:process>` root and no
23	namespace declarations. Author this shell yourself. The canvas import detector
24	(`exporter.ts`) requires: a root `<…:definitions>` carrying a BPMN-spec
25	namespace, at least one `<bpmn:process>`, and (to render) a
26	`<bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>`.
27	
28	```xml
29	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
30	<bpmn:definitions
31	    xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL"
32	    xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI"
33	    xmlns:dc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC"
34	    xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI"
35	    xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
36	    xmlns:uipath="http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn"
37	    id="Definitions_1" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn"
38	    exporter="UiPath (https://bpmn.uipath.com)" exporterVersion="1.0">
39	  <bpmn:process id="Process_1" isExecutable="true">
40	    <!-- variables, flow nodes, sequence flows -->
41	  </bpmn:process>
42	  <bpmndi:BPMNDiagram id="Diagram_1">
43	    <bpmndi:BPMNPlane id="Plane_1" bpmnElement="Process_1">
44	      <!-- one BPMNShape per node, one BPMNEdge per flow -->
45	    </bpmndi:BPMNPlane>
46	  </bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>
47	</bpmn:definitions>
48	```
49	
50	The `uipath:` namespace URI is exactly `http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn`. All
51	`uipath:*` tags inside `extensionElements` are lower-camelCase
52	(`uipath:activity`, `uipath:variables`, `uipath:loopCharacteristics`, …).
53	
54	Every `uipath:activity` / `uipath:event` / `uipath:mapping` carries its node
55	type as a **child** element — `<uipath:type value="<Type>" version="v1" />` —
56	never as a `type=` attribute on the wrapper. This holds both for templated nodes
57	and for any shell you author or preserve by hand.
58	
59	XML comments must not contain `--` (double-hyphen): it is invalid XML and the
60	file will fail to parse. Never paste CLI commands or flags
61	(`--output`, `--connection-id`) into `<!-- … -->`. Keep comments minimal.
62	
63	## A complete minimal file (author from this, not from fixtures)
64	
65	This is the whole shape — variables, an entry point, one node, a branch, and
66	the diagram — in one valid file. Author from this skeleton plus the registry
67	templates for your nodes. **Do not read the validator's `test/fixtures/` to
68	infer the pattern**; those are validator test data, and reading them is the
69	main reason authoring runs out of time. Swap the `scriptTask` payload for the
70	registry `xmlTemplate` of whatever node you need.
71	
72	```xml
73	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
74	<bpmn:definitions
75	    xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL"
76	    xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI"
77	    xmlns:dc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC"
78	    xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI"
79	    xmlns:uipath="http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn"
80	    id="Definitions_1" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn"
81	    exporter="UiPath (https://bpmn.uipath.com)" exporterVersion="1.0">
82	  <bpmn:process id="Process_1" isExecutable="true">
83	    <bpmn:extensionElements>
84	      <uipath:migrationVersion version="11.5" />
85	      <uipath:variables version="v1">
86	        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Amount" name="Amount" type="number" />
87	        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Tier" name="Tier" type="string" />
88	      </uipath:variables>
89	    </bpmn:extensionElements>
90	    <bpmn:startEvent id="Start_1" name="Start"><bpmn:outgoing>Flow_1</bpmn:outgoing></bpmn:startEvent>
91	    <bpmn:scriptTask id="Task_Tier" name="Classify" scriptFormat="JavaScript">
92	      <bpmn:extensionElements>
93	        <uipath:scriptVersion value="v3" />
94	        <uipath:mapping version="v1">
95	          <uipath:type value="BPMN.ScriptTask" version="v1" />
96	          <uipath:input name="args"><![CDATA[{"amount":"=vars.Var_Amount"}]]></uipath:input>
97	          <uipath:output name="tier" type="string" var="Var_Tier" source="=result.response" />
98	        </uipath:mapping>
99	      </bpmn:extensionElements>
100	      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_1</bpmn:incoming><bpmn:outgoing>Flow_2</bpmn:outgoing>
101	      <bpmn:script><![CDATA[return { response: amount > 1000 ? "high" : "low" };]]></bpmn:script>
102	    </bpmn:scriptTask>
103	    <bpmn:exclusiveGateway id="Gw_1" name="Tier?" default="Flow_Low">
104	      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_2</bpmn:incoming>
105	      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_High</bpmn:outgoing><bpmn:outgoing>Flow_Low</bpmn:outgoing>
106	    </bpmn:exclusiveGateway>
107	    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_High" name="High"><bpmn:incoming>Flow_High</bpmn:incoming></bpmn:endEvent>
108	    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_Low" name="Low"><bpmn:incoming>Flow_Low</bpmn:incoming></bpmn:endEvent>
109	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_1" sourceRef="Start_1" targetRef="Task_Tier" />
110	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_2" sourceRef="Task_Tier" targetRef="Gw_1" />
111	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_High" sourceRef="Gw_1" targetRef="End_High">
112	      <bpmn:conditionExpression xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression"
113	        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">=vars.Var_Tier == "high"</bpmn:conditionExpression>
114	    </bpmn:sequenceFlow>
115	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_Low" sourceRef="Gw_1" targetRef="End_Low" />
116	  </bpmn:process>
117	  <bpmndi:BPMNDiagram id="Diagram_1">
118	    <bpmndi:BPMNPlane id="Plane_1" bpmnElement="Process_1">
119	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Start" bpmnElement="Start_1"><dc:Bounds x="160" y="100" width="36" height="36" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
120	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Task" bpmnElement="Task_Tier"><dc:Bounds x="250" y="78" width="100" height="80" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
121	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Gw" bpmnElement="Gw_1"><dc:Bounds x="410" y="93" width="50" height="50" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
122	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_High" bpmnElement="End_High"><dc:Bounds x="520" y="40" width="36" height="36" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
123	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Low" bpmnElement="End_Low"><dc:Bounds x="520" y="160" width="36" height="36" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
124	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_1" bpmnElement="Flow_1"><di:waypoint x="196" y="118" /><di:waypoint x="250" y="118" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
125	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_2" bpmnElement="Flow_2"><di:waypoint x="350" y="118" /><di:waypoint x="410" y="118" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
126	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_High" bpmnElement="Flow_High"><di:waypoint x="435" y="93" /><di:waypoint x="435" y="58" /><di:waypoint x="520" y="58" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
127	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_Low" bpmnElement="Flow_Low"><di:waypoint x="435" y="143" /><di:waypoint x="435" y="178" /><di:waypoint x="520" y="178" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
128	    </bpmndi:BPMNPlane>
129	  </bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>
130	</bpmn:definitions>
131	```
132	
133	## Variables (`BPMN.Variables`)
134	
135	Declare root variables with the `BPMN.Variables` registry template attached to
136	the process via `extensionElements`, or use the canvas `<uipath:variables>`
137	block directly. Variable bodies are CDATA. Reference variables in expressions as
138	`vars.<id>` — see [expression-authoring.md](expression-authoring.md).
139	Sub-process-scoped variables go in that sub-process's own `<uipath:variables>`.
140	
141	## Script tasks (`BPMN.ScriptTask`) — Jint runtime contract
142	
143	`bpmn:scriptTask scriptFormat="JavaScript"` runs under **Jint**, not Node.js or
144	a browser. The mapping payload comes from the `BPMN.ScriptTask` registry
145	template, but the runtime contract is fixed:
146	
147	- Only these helpers exist: `uipath.aggregate`, `uipath._aggregate`,
148	  `uipath._pipe`, and a no-op `console`. No npm packages, filesystem, network,
149	  browser globals, or long-running async behavior. Execution envelope is ~64 MB
150	  / 30 s.
151	- Set `uipath:scriptVersion value="v3"` for new scripts; preserve an imported
152	  `value="v2"`. For v2+ the script returns JSON under `response`.
153	- Mapped `args` fields are read as **top-level identifiers** in the script body
154	  (`amount`, not `args.amount`); the input mapping itself stays `name="args"`
155	  and maps each field by variable id (`=vars.Var_Amount`).
156	- Map the return back through `source="=result.response"` (scalar) or
157	  `source="=result.response.<field>"` (object field); `var` points at a declared
158	  variable id (do not put the target id in `name`).
159	
160	```xml
161	<bpmn:scriptTask id="Task_RiskScore" name="Risk Score" scriptFormat="JavaScript">
162	  <bpmn:extensionElements>
163	    <uipath:scriptVersion value="v3" />
164	    <uipath:mapping version="v1">
165	      <uipath:type value="BPMN.ScriptTask" version="v1" />
166	      <uipath:input name="args"><![CDATA[{"amount":"=vars.Var_Amount","daysOverdue":"=vars.Var_DaysOverdue"}]]></uipath:input>
167	      <uipath:output name="riskScore" type="number" var="Var_RiskScore" source="=result.response" />
168	    </uipath:mapping>
169	  </bpmn:extensionElements>
170	  <bpmn:script><![CDATA[
171	var score = amount * 0.01 + daysOverdue * 2;
172	return { response: score };
173	]]></bpmn:script>
174	</bpmn:scriptTask>
175	```
176	
177	## Sequence flows, conditions, and gateway defaults (REGISTRY GAP)
178	
179	The registry never emits `<bpmn:sequenceFlow>`, conditions, or the gateway
180	`default` attribute. Author all of them.
181	
182	- A flow: `<bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_1" sourceRef="A" targetRef="B" />`. The
183	  source/target nodes must also list `<bpmn:incoming>`/`<bpmn:outgoing>`
184	  (the registry templates leave `{incomingEdge}`/`{outgoingEdge}` placeholders
185	  for exactly these).
186	- Conditional flow body: `<bpmn:conditionExpression xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">=vars.Var_X == "approved"</bpmn:conditionExpression>`.
187	  The canvas normalizes the body to start with `=` — always lead with `=`.
188	- Gateway default flow: set `default="Flow_else"` on the gateway element, and
189	  give that flow no condition.
190	
191	## Gateways
192	
193	Author these gateway types for new BPMN: `bpmn:ExclusiveGateway`,
194	`bpmn:ParallelGateway`, `bpmn:InclusiveGateway`, `bpmn:EventBasedGateway`.
195	`bpmn:ComplexGateway` round-trips structurally but is **preserve-only** — do not
196	generate it for new authoring (see [Do not generate for new
197	authoring](#do-not-generate-for-new-authoring-preserve-on-round-trip-only)).
198	
199	- **Exclusive (XOR)**: each non-default outgoing flow needs a
200	  `conditionExpression`; exactly one outgoing flow is the `default`. (Validator
201	  rule `MISSING_CONDITION_EXPRESSION`.)
202	- **Parallel (AND)**: fork = one in, many out; join = many in, one out. No
203	  conditions.
204	- **Inclusive (OR)**: conditions on outgoing flows; multiple may be taken.
205	- **Event-based**: routes to the first of several catch events / receive tasks
206	  to fire; its outgoing flows target intermediate catch events or receive tasks.
207	- A gateway with exactly one incoming and one outgoing flow is rejected
208	  (`SUPERFLUOUS_GATEWAY`). Activities/events must not have more than one
209	  incoming flow — join with a gateway, not a "fake join" (`FAKE_JOIN`).
210	
211	## Events and the event-definition matrix
212	
213	`bpmn-spec.json` `bpmnElements.events` enumerates which event definitions each
214	event element can carry on **round-trip**. For **new authoring**, only the
215	**none**, **Message**, **Timer**, **Error** (on end + boundary), and
216	**Terminate** (on end events only) definitions are generated. Conditional,
217	Signal, Escalation, Compensate, Cancel, Link, multiple, and parallel-multiple
218	definitions are **preserve-only** — keep them when imported, but do not generate
219	them for new BPMN (see [Do not generate for new
220	authoring](#do-not-generate-for-new-authoring-preserve-on-round-trip-only)).
221	
222	The matrix below is the round-trip acceptance per element; **preserve-only**
223	marks definitions that the skill keeps but does not author for new files.
224	
225	| Event element | Authorable | Preserve-only (round-trip) |
226	| --- | --- | --- |
227	| `bpmn:StartEvent` | none, Message, Timer | Conditional, Signal |
228	| `bpmn:IntermediateThrowEvent` | none, Message | Escalation, Signal, Link, Compensate |
229	| `bpmn:IntermediateCatchEvent` | Message, Timer | Escalation, Signal, Conditional, Link, Compensate |
230	| `bpmn:EndEvent` | none, Message, Error, Terminate | Escalation, Compensate, Signal |
231	| `bpmn:BoundaryEvent` | Message, Timer, Error | Escalation, Conditional, Signal, Compensate |
232	
233	Payload shapes the canvas serializes:
234	
235	- **Timer**: `<bpmn:timerEventDefinition><bpmn:timeDuration xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">PT30M</bpmn:timeDuration></bpmn:timerEventDefinition>`
236	  (or `timeDate` / `timeCycle`). Static durations must be valid ISO-8601;
237	  week designators (`PnW`) are unsupported. Expression-mode is allowed and
238	  accepts either prefix — `=…` or `@…`.
239	- **Message**: `<bpmn:messageEventDefinition messageRef="Message_1" />` with a
240	  `<bpmn:message id="Message_1" name="…"/>` declared at definitions level. The
241	  Maestro internal-message events (`Maestro.ReceiveMessageEvent` /
242	  `Maestro.SendMessageEvent`) carry the `uipath:event` payload **and** a bare
243	  `<bpmn:messageEventDefinition />` (see their registry templates).
244	- **Error**: `<bpmn:errorEventDefinition errorRef="Error_1" />` with a
245	  `<bpmn:error id="Error_1" name="…" errorCode="…"/>` at definitions level. An
246	  error end event with no `errorRef` fails to parse at runtime
247	  (`ERROR_END_EVENT_MISSING_EXCEPTION`); an error referenced by a boundary event
248	  must declare an `errorCode` (`ERROR_BOUNDARY_EVENT_REQUIRES_ERROR_CODE`).
249	- **Terminate** (end events only): emit the bare
250	  `<bpmn:terminateEventDefinition />`.
251	
252	Preserve-only payloads — keep these when imported, but do not author them for
253	new files:
254	
255	- **Signal**: `<bpmn:signalEventDefinition signalRef="Signal_1" />` with a
256	  definitions-level `<bpmn:signal/>`.
257	- **Escalation**: `<bpmn:escalationEventDefinition escalationRef="Escalation_1" />`
258	  with a `<bpmn:escalation id="Escalation_1" name="…" escalationCode="…"/>`
259	  declared at definitions level (parallel to message/error/signal).
260	- **Conditional / Link / Compensate**: the bare definition element; the canvas
261	  round-trips it.
262	
263	### Boundary events (REGISTRY GAP for `attachedToRef` / `cancelActivity`)
264	
265	A boundary event attaches to an activity and catches an event on it. The
266	registry exposes no boundary template; author it:
267	
268	```xml
269	<bpmn:boundaryEvent id="Boundary_Timeout" attachedToRef="Task_DoWork" cancelActivity="true">
270	  <bpmn:timerEventDefinition>
271	    <bpmn:timeDuration xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">PT15M</bpmn:timeDuration>
272	  </bpmn:timerEventDefinition>
273	  <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_OnTimeout</bpmn:outgoing>
274	</bpmn:boundaryEvent>
275	```
276	
277	- `attachedToRef` = id of the activity it sits on. The boundary event and that
278	  activity must be `flowElements` of the same parent scope.
279	- `cancelActivity="true"` = interrupting (default); `cancelActivity="false"` =
280	  non-interrupting. Per the spec, non-interrupting is available for
281	  Message/Timer/Escalation/Conditional/Signal — **not** Error or Compensate.
282	- Only one catch-all (no `errorRef`) error boundary event per task, and no two
283	  error boundary events with the same error code on one task
284	  (`MULTIPLE_CATCH_ALL_BOUNDARY_EVENTS_ON_TASK`,
285	  `DUPLICATE_ERROR_BOUNDARY_EVENT_ON_TASK`).
286	
287	### Retry and error mapping (REGISTRY GAP)
288	
289	UiPath-specific retry and error-mapping metadata live inside an activity's
290	`extensionElements`. The error **code** lives on the declared
291	`bpmn:error errorCode="…"`; `uipath:*` elements reference it through `errorRef`.
292	
293	```xml
294	<uipath:retry maxRetryCount="2" retryBackoff="PT30S" retryBackoffType="exponential"
295	              maxDuration="PT5M" exponentialBase="2" retryAllErrors="false">
296	  <uipath:errorDefinition errorRef="Error_ServiceUnavailable" />
297	</uipath:retry>
298	<uipath:errorMapping version="v1">
299	  <uipath:error id="Mapped_ServiceUnavailable" errorRef="Error_ServiceUnavailable"
300	                priority="1" condition="=vars.error.code == "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE""
301	                detail="Service unavailable" retryable="true" />
302	</uipath:errorMapping>
303	```
304	
305	- `uipath:retry` attributes: `maxRetryCount`, `retryBackoff`, `retryBackoffType`,
306	  `maxDuration`, `exponentialBase`, `retryAllErrors`. Do not use stale aliases
307	  (`maxAttempts`, `interval`).
308	- `uipath:error` (mapping) fields: `id`, `errorRef`, `priority`, `condition`,
309	  `detail`, `retryable` (`true`/`false`). Conditions read the runtime error via
310	  `vars.error` and contain no assignments. Do not put `code=` on `uipath:error`;
311	  model the code on `bpmn:error errorCode` and reference via `errorRef`.
312	
313	## Subprocess, call activity, event subprocess (REGISTRY GAP for structure)
314	
315	- **SubProcess** (`bpmn:SubProcess`): a container with its own nested
316	  `flowElements` (start event, nodes, end event) and its own scoped
317	  `<uipath:variables>`. Variants: `collapsed`, `expanded`, `eventSubprocess`.
318	  The shape carries `isExpanded` for the collapsed/expanded distinction.
319	- **Event subprocess**: a `bpmn:SubProcess` with `triggeredByEvent="true"`. It
320	  must have **exactly one** start event, and that start event **must carry an
321	  event definition** (with `isInterrupting`) — a blank start event is invalid for
322	  an event subprocess.
323	- **Call activity** (`bpmn:CallActivity`): invokes a *separate* Maestro
324	  instance. The registry provides the `uipath:activity` payload for the
325	  Orchestrator agentic/case-management call-activity types
326	  (`Orchestrator.StartAgenticProcess[Async]`, `…CaseMgmtProcess[Async]`). A
327	  plain BPMN `calledElement` round-trips but is not specially authored by the
328	  canvas layer.
329	- Each scope (process or sub-process) may have at most one blank (untyped) start
330	  event (`MULTIPLE_BLANK_START_EVENTS`).
331	
332	> SubProcess scopes operations *within the same instance*; CallActivity invokes
333	> a *separate* Maestro instance. Do not conflate them.
334	
335	## Multi-instance / loop characteristics (REGISTRY GAP — canvas supports it)
336	
337	The registry spec enumerates **no** multi-instance or loop markers
338	(`grep` for `multiInstance`/`loopCharacteristics` in `bpmn-spec.json` returns
339	nothing). This is a genuine registry gap. The Studio Web canvas, however, **does**
340	serialize them (`elements/nodes.ts`), so author them from the canvas contract:
341	
342	```xml
343	<bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics isSequential="true">
344	  <bpmn:completionCondition xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">=vars.Var_Done</bpmn:completionCondition>
345	  <bpmn:extensionElements>
346	    <uipath:loopCharacteristics
347	        inputCollection="=vars.Var_Items" inputElement="item" />
348	  </bpmn:extensionElements>
349	</bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics>
350	```
351	
352	- `isSequential="true"` = one at a time; `false` = parallel.
353	- The collection/item binding lives in the `uipath:loopCharacteristics`
354	  extension (`inputCollection`, `inputElement`), **not** in `loopCardinality`
355	  (the canvas never reads `loopCardinality`).
356	- The loop element **must declare `inputElement`** — do not rely on reading a
357	  bare `iterator`/`iterator.item` downstream without it. For a multi-instance
358	  **subprocess** body, bind `inputElement="iterator[0]"` on
359	  `uipath:loopCharacteristics` and pass the current item into body activities
360	  with `=iterator[0].item`. Do not assume a bare alias such as `=currentItem` is
361	  in scope inside a marker subprocess body unless the file already uses it.
362	- Inside the body, read the current item with the `iterator` namespace — see
363	  [expression-authoring.md](expression-authoring.md).
364	- `bpmn:standardLoopCharacteristics` is also recognized (no uipath extension).
365	
366	Because the registry exposes no template for this, treat it as a documented
367	authoring path backed by the canvas serializer, and tell the user it is a
368	registry gap if they ask why no `registry get` covers it.
369	
370	## Do not generate for new authoring (preserve on round-trip only)
371	
372	These structures are **not** authored for new Maestro BPMN. If they appear in an
373	imported or brownfield file, preserve them and report that the skill cannot
374	safely regenerate or normalize them. Planned / preview / TBD statuses count as
375	unsupported for generation until current tooling confirms them.
376	
377	- Gateway: `bpmn:complexGateway`.
378	- Tasks / containers: `bpmn:manualTask`, `bpmn:adHocSubProcess`,
379	  `bpmn:transaction`.
380	- Event definitions: `conditionalEventDefinition`, `signalEventDefinition`,
381	  `escalationEventDefinition`, `compensateEventDefinition`,
382	  `cancelEventDefinition`, `linkEventDefinition`, multiple, and
383	  parallel-multiple event definitions.
384	- Markers: standard-loop and compensation markers (use only documented
385	  multi-instance parallel/sequential metadata for new loops).
386	- Terminate is supported **only** on end events — not on start, boundary,
387	  intermediate-catch, or intermediate-throw events.
388	- Preserve-only `uipath:*` extension payloads: keep these when imported, and when
389	  a file legitimately needs one, reproduce the shape (with synthetic, public-safe
390	  contents) rather than inventing a new one.
391	  - **Typed activity/event shells always use the lowercase `<uipath:activity>` /
392	    `<uipath:event>` wrapper with a `<uipath:type value="<Type>" version="v1" />`
393	    child** — including types not served by the live registry (e.g.
394	    `Maestro.CasePlanScheduler`, `Maestro.CaseManagerGuardrails`,
395	    `Maestro.CaseRulesEvaluator`). Do **not** substitute the capital-`A`
396	    `<uipath:Activity>` element for a typed shell.
397	  - The capital-`A` `<uipath:Activity>` element is a **separate** generic
398	    preserve-only payload (its own element, not a wrapper for typed shells).
399	  - `uipath:caseManagement` is a versioned body-string element —
400	    `<uipath:caseManagement version="v1">…synthetic payload…</uipath:caseManagement>`.
401	  - `<uipath:scriptVersion value="v2" />` is legacy: author `v3` for new scripts,
402	    preserve `v2` where it already exists.
403	
404	## Diagram interchange — `bpmndi` (REGISTRY GAP — always generated)
405	
406	The registry emits no diagram. Import is **diagram-driven**: the canvas builds
407	nodes from `BPMNShape`s and edges from `BPMNEdge`s, not by walking
408	`flowElements`. **A node with no shape is invisible; a flow with no edge is
409	dropped.** You must generate the full `BPMNDiagram` yourself.
410	
411	- One `<bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_<nodeId>" bpmnElement="<nodeId>">` per node, with
412	  `<dc:Bounds x= y= width= height= />`. SubProcess shapes carry `isExpanded`.
413	- One `<bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="BPMNEdge_<flowId>" bpmnElement="<flowId>">` per
414	  sequence flow, with `<di:waypoint x= y= />` points.
415	- Lay nodes out left-to-right with non-overlapping bounds. Typical sizes: tasks
416	  100×80, events 36×36, gateways 50×50.
417	
418	Example:
419	
420	```xml
421	<bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_StartEvent_1" bpmnElement="StartEvent_1">
422	  <dc:Bounds x="160" y="100" width="36" height="36" />
423	</bpmndi:BPMNShape>
424	<bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="BPMNEdge_Flow_1" bpmnElement="Flow_1">
425	  <di:waypoint x="196" y="118" />
426	  <di:waypoint x="260" y="118" />
427	</bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
428	```
429	
430	## Editing operations
431	
432	Safe, surgical edits on an existing `.bpmn` (preserve content you did not author
433	— see [SKILL.md](../SKILL.md#editing-an-existing-bpmn-preserve-what-you-did-not-author)):
434	
435	- **Add / delete / reconnect a node**: add the element with a stable id and its
436	  `<bpmn:incoming>`/`<bpmn:outgoing>` refs, add the sequence-flow elements in the
437	  owning scope, and add/update its `BPMNShape` and edge waypoints. On delete,
438	  remove orphaned flows and DI edges and recheck entry-point variables, output
439	  mappings, and binding references.
440	- **Insert a gateway**: split the existing sequence flow into an incoming and an
441	  outgoing flow, add conditions to the outgoing flows plus one `default`, add a
442	  matching join only if branches actually need synchronization, then re-waypoint
443	  the diagram (gateway shape + all edges).
444	- **Move logic into a subprocess**: move only elements that share a valid scope,
445	  re-scope their variables, recreate legal subprocess flow boundaries, and add a
446	  second diagram plane for the subprocess so nested content renders.
447	- **Add an entry point**: use a root-level start event, add a stable unique
448	  `uipath:entryPointId`, and declare input/output variables whose `elementId`
449	  matches that start event.
450	
451	Do not patch generated JSON to fix source behavior — change the `.bpmn` and
452	regenerate. For `Intsvc.*` activities/triggers, hand editing to CLI enrichment.
453	
454	### Edit red-flags
455	
456	Re-check after any edit:
457	
458	- A diagram plane references a missing process, collaboration, or subprocess.
459	- A rendered element lacks a shape or its flow lacks an edge/waypoint.
460	- An entry-point variable points at the wrong start event.
461	- A `uipath:context` value references a missing binding.
462	- A topology edit rewrote unrelated `uipath:*` extension XML.
463	- An Integration Service element carries hand-authored connection details.
464	
465	## Validation
466	
467	There is **no** `uip maestro bpmn validate` CLI command. The skill ships its own
468	offline validator that reconstructs the PO.Frontend Node/Edge/CanvasState model
469	from the parsed BPMN and runs **every** canvas validation rule — run it as the
470	primary check:
471	
472	```bash
473	cd skills/uipath-maestro-bpmn/validator && npm install --silent
474	node validate-bpmn.mjs <file.bpmn>   # prints VALID and exits 0, or prints errors and exits 1
475	```
476	
477	`VALID` / exit 0 means the document passes all rules. Any other output lists the
478	blocking errors (gateway/condition, fake-join, superfluous-gateway, error
479	end/boundary event, timer-duration, single-blank-start, variable-reference, and
480	IS-connector checks). See [validator/README.md](../validator/README.md).
481	
482	If Node is unavailable, fall back to a well-formed-XML parse plus the structural
483	checklist below — it mirrors the same blocking rules:
484	
485	```bash
486	python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('<file.bpmn>')"
487	```
488	
489	Then walk the structural checklist:
490	
491	1. Root is `<…:definitions>` with the BPMN + `uipath` namespaces.
492	2. Exactly one `<bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>` with a shape per node and an edge per flow.
493	3. Every `sourceRef`/`targetRef`/`attachedToRef`/`*Ref` resolves to a declared id.
494	4. Each XOR gateway: non-default flows have conditions; exactly one default.
495	5. No activity/event has more than one incoming flow.
496	6. Each event subprocess has exactly one start event, and it carries an event
497	   definition (with `isInterrupting`).
498	7. Every `vars.<id>` reference resolves to a declared variable.
499	8. Each `uipath:*` payload was produced from a `registry get` template, not
500	   hand-written.
501

1	# Project Layout
2	
3	Maestro BPMN Process Orchestration projects use BPMN XML as source and generated JSON files as package/runtime metadata.
4	
5	## Canonical source files
6	
7	- `<project>.bpmn` - BPMN process source and UiPath extension XML. For a new
8	  project, keep the BPMN source basename exactly aligned with the project
9	  directory/name. For example, `InvoiceTriageBpmn/InvoiceTriageBpmn.bpmn`, not
10	  `InvoiceTriageBpmn/invoice-triage-bpmn.bpmn`.
11	- `project.uiproj` - UiPath project metadata. Keep it in the same project
12	  directory as the main BPMN file: `InvoiceTriageBpmn/project.uiproj`, not next
13	  to the project directory.
14	
15	For a new local project, create a single project directory and place source
16	files under it:
17	
18	```text
19	ProjectName/
20	  ProjectName.bpmn
21	  project.uiproj
22	```
23	
24	## Generated or CLI-managed package files
25	
26	- `bindings_v2.json` - resource bindings generated or enriched from BPMN and registry/connection metadata.
27	- `entry-points.json` - runnable start-event entry points and input/output schemas.
28	- `operate.json` - runtime/package metadata.
29	- `package-descriptor.json` - package manifest mapping generated files and BPMN content.
30	
31	Treat these JSON files as derived unless a CLI contract explicitly identifies a field as user-authored. For source fixes, edit BPMN or rerun CLI enrichment rather than patching generated output by hand.
32	
33	Local packaging requires the generated metadata set to exist. In particular,
34	`uip maestro bpmn pack <project-path> <OutputDir> --output json` consumes
35	`package-descriptor.json`; it does not create a missing descriptor from only
36	the BPMN and `project.uiproj`.
37	
38	For the regeneration and drift-check contract, see [local-metadata-regeneration-guide.md](local-metadata-regeneration-guide.md).
39	
40	## Package content
41	
42	A Process Orchestration package content folder contains:
43	
44	- One or more `.bpmn` files.
45	- `bindings_v2.json`.
46	- `entry-points.json`.
47	- `operate.json`.
48	- `package-descriptor.json`.
49	
50	The package descriptor maps BPMN and generated JSON files under `content/`. The entry point file path references the BPMN file and start event, using the root start event's unique entry point ID.
51	
52	## Authoring boundary
53	
54	Authoring owns local files and local validation. It can create or edit BPMN source, preserve existing generated files for comparison, and run validation/generation commands. It stops before upload, debug, publish, or run unless the user explicitly consents.
55	
56	Operate owns cloud-side side effects: upload, publish, deploy, debug, process run, instance lifecycle, and cloud resource refresh.
57	
58	Diagnose owns post-run inspection: incidents, variables, element executions, deployed assets, traces, and correlation back to BPMN element IDs.
59

1	# Local Metadata Regeneration
2	
3	Use this guide when BPMN source changed and local package metadata must be refreshed or verified before packaging, upload, debug, publish, or deploy.
4	
5	## Ownership
6	
7	- `.bpmn` is the source of record for process structure, root variables, root bindings, entry point IDs, mappings, diagrams, and documented non-Integration-Service UiPath XML.
8	- `entry-points.json`, `bindings_v2.json`, `operate.json`, and `package-descriptor.json` are derived package metadata unless a CLI contract explicitly marks a field as user-authored.
9	- Connector-backed or dynamically schematized `Intsvc.*` activity and event payloads are executable only after registry-backed enrichment supplies connector metadata, connection binding references, dynamic schemas, and generated package resources. Confirmed plain connectionless HTTP follows the documented pass-2 authoring recipe instead.
10	
11	## Regeneration Inputs
12	
13	Local regeneration reads:
14	
15	- Root-level `bpmn:startEvent` elements with `uipath:entryPointId`.
16	- Root `uipath:variables` for entry point input/output schemas.
17	- Root `uipath:bindings` for package resources.
18	- Enriched `uipath:activity` and `uipath:event` payloads for `Intsvc.*` context fields, request payloads, output mappings, and schemas.
19	- The project main file from `project.uiproj` or the selected BPMN file.
20	
21	Do not derive metadata from stale package files first. Use existing generated files only as a drift comparison or as CLI-owned enrichment input when the CLI explicitly supports that workflow.
22	
23	## Safe Local Workflow
24	
25	1. Edit `.bpmn` first.
26	2. Run local validation for XML, diagrams, entry point IDs, variables, mappings, binding references, and package metadata drift.
27	3. Before running `pack`, verify the project directory contains the full local
28	   metadata set: `project.uiproj`, `operate.json`, `entry-points.json`,
29	   `bindings_v2.json`, and `package-descriptor.json`. The pack command
30	   consumes these files; it does not synthesize a missing package descriptor.
31	4. If generated package JSON is stale, regenerate it with the supported local
32	   CLI path. If no generator is available for a local-only synthetic project,
33	   write the minimal placeholder-safe shape below before packing. For
34	   package-shape verification, use the local pack command and request JSON
35	   output when parsing command results:
36	
37	   ```bash
38	   uip maestro bpmn pack <project-path> <OutputDir> --output json
39	   ```
40	
41	5. Inspect the package or generated content for:
42	   - `entry-points.json` entries matching root start events and schemas.
43	   - `bindings_v2.json` resources matching root bindings and enriched connector metadata.
44	   - `operate.json` pointing at the intended BPMN file with `ProcessOrchestration` content type.
45	   - `package-descriptor.json` entries for the BPMN file and generated JSON under `content/`.
46	6. If the installed CLI cannot regenerate a needed file in place, keep the generated file stale only as a known blocker and report the exact unsupported step.
47	
48	Packaging is local and authoring-safe. Upload, publish, deploy, debug, and run are cloud or runtime actions and still require explicit user consent.
49	
50	## Minimal Local Metadata Shape
51	
52	When a local-only synthetic project needs package files and the CLI cannot
53	regenerate them in place, use this placeholder-safe shape before running
54	`uip maestro bpmn pack`. Replace only the BPMN file name and start event id;
55	do not invent `contentFiles` as a substitute for `content`.
56	
57	`project.uiproj`:
58	
59	```json
60	{
61	  "projectVersion": "1.0.0",
62	  "ProjectType": "ProcessOrchestration",
63	  "Name": "SyntheticProject",
64	  "main": "SyntheticProject.bpmn"
65	}
66	```
67	
68	`operate.json`:
69	
70	```json
71	{
72	  "main": "SyntheticProject.bpmn",
73	  "contentType": "ProcessOrchestration"
74	}
75	```
76	
77	`entry-points.json`:
78	
79	```json
80	{
81	  "entryPoints": [
82	    {
83	      "id": "Entry_ManualStart",
84	      "filePath": "/content/SyntheticProject.bpmn#Start_Manual",
85	      "inputSchema": { "type": "object", "properties": {} },
86	      "outputSchema": { "type": "object", "properties": {} }
87	    }
88	  ]
89	}
90	```
91	
92	`bindings_v2.json`:
93	
94	```json
95	{
96	  "version": "2.0",
97	  "resources": []
98	}
99	```
100	
101	This empty resource file is a package-shape placeholder only for projects with
102	no generated resource dependencies. It is not evidence that dependency refresh
103	imported an external process, queue, connector, or agent.
104	
105	`package-descriptor.json`:
106	
107	```json
108	{
109	  "content": [
110	    "content/SyntheticProject.bpmn",
111	    "content/bindings_v2.json",
112	    "content/entry-points.json",
113	    "content/operate.json"
114	  ]
115	}
116	```
117	
118	## Entry Point Rules
119	
120	For each root start event with `uipath:entryPointId`, generated `entry-points.json` must include:
121	
122	- `id` equal to the `uipath:entryPointId` value.
123	- `filePath` equal to `/content/<bpmn-file>#<start-event-id>`.
124	- `inputSchema` from root input variables whose `elementId` matches the start event.
125	- `outputSchema` from root output variables.
126	
127	JSON schema variables use their CDATA body as the property schema. Strip `$schema` from generated package schemas. Other primitive variables map by type, such as `string`, `integer`, `number`, `boolean`, `array`, `object`, or `json`.
128	
129	## Binding Rules
130	
131	Generated `bindings_v2.json` must be a top-level object with
132	`"version": "2.0"` and a `resources` array. Do not use a bare resource array, a
133	single resource object, or an unversioned `{ "resources": [] }` object; those
134	shapes are not the package contract consumed by solution resources refresh.
135	
136	The resource array has two consumers with different tolerance:
137	
138	- Local/package binding expressions may need id-addressable entries that mirror
139	  root `uipath:binding` IDs.
140	- `uip solution resources refresh` reads the same `resources` array and imports
141	  concrete dependencies only when it contains parseable resource entries.
142	  Process resources should come from CLI generation or fixture-backed binding
143	  entries with `id`, `kind`, `name`, `resourceKey`, `metadata`, `resource`,
144	  `resourceSubType`, and, for name/folder-path binding pairs,
145	  `propertyAttribute`.
146	
147	When an executable BPMN depends on remote Orchestrator processes, include
148	generated process binding resources before refresh so it can import the
149	process/package resources and write debug overwrites. If resource dependencies
150	are expected, verify that refresh produced matching generated resource files or
151	explicitly report that no dependency resources were imported.
152	
153	Generated id-addressable entries should preserve:
154	
155	- `id`, `name`, kind/type, and `resourceKey`.
156	- `metadata.BindingsVersion` for the source binding version.
157	- `metadata.DisplayLabel` from the binding display name.
158	- `metadata.SubType` from the binding resource subtype or type.
159	- Connector metadata, parent resource keys, and solution support fields supplied by Integration Service enrichment.
160	
161	If multiple BPMN elements share a connector connection binding, regenerate or validate deduplication through the CLI instead of copying a resource entry by hand.
162	
163	## Integration Service Enrichment
164	
165	Before a connector element is executable, enrichment must make these fields agree:
166	
167	- Every `Intsvc.*` `connection`, `trigger`, object/property, or resource context value references an existing root binding with `=bindings.<id>`.
168	- The referenced package resource exists in `bindings_v2.json`.
169	- Connector-specific package metadata agrees with the `connectorKey` in the enriched payload.
170	- Trigger property resources carry the parent trigger resource key when required by the connector shape.
171	- Activity payloads include required operation context and generated request/input schema data when the selected operation requires it.
172	- Output mappings target declared variables and dynamic output schemas are generated by the enrichment tool.
173	
174	If enrichment is unavailable, leave the BPMN element as draft intent. Do not hand-author real connection IDs, tenant resource keys, connector payloads, or dynamic schemas.
175	
176	## Drift Handling
177	
178	- If `entry-points.json` differs from root variables or start event IDs, fix the BPMN source first, then regenerate.
179	- If `bindings_v2.json` differs from root bindings or `Intsvc.*` context references, rerun enrichment/generation.
180	- If `operate.json` or `package-descriptor.json` points at the wrong BPMN file, refresh package metadata through the CLI path.
181	- Do not commit private IDs, tenant URLs, connection IDs, folder keys, or copied customer payloads while resolving drift.
182

1	# Registry workflow
2	
3	Every `uipath:*` payload in a Maestro `.bpmn` comes from the registry — never
4	from prose, never hand-written. This file is the loop for turning user intent
5	into registry-backed XML.
6	
7	## 1. Sync and discover
8	
9	```bash
10	uip maestro bpmn registry pull            # sync + cache (login for connectors/processes)
11	uip maestro bpmn registry list --limit -1 --output json   # all extension types
12	uip maestro bpmn registry search <keyword> --output json  # find a type by intent
13	uip is connections list --all-folders --output json   # live IS connections (all folders)
14	```
15	
16	Map the user's intent to an extension type from the list. Confirm the choice
17	with the user (and the specific connection / process / queue) before authoring.
18	**Never fabricate an identifier** — see [cli-conventions.md](cli-conventions.md).
19	
20	**Connection discovery must be exhaustive.** Always pass `--all-folders` to
21	`uip is connections list` — connections live in many folders and a folder-scoped
22	listing silently misses them. An empty or unmatched result from a missing
23	`--all-folders`, or from a connector key guessed from a brand name rather than
24	found via `registry search`, is a **false negative** — never conclude "no
25	connection exists" or ask the user to create one until you have searched the
26	registry for the real connector key and listed across all folders.
27	
28	`registry list` returns three buckets in `Data`: `ExtensionTypes` (the OOTB
29	extension types, always available), `Connectors` and `Processes` (only after
30	`uip login`). Each extension-type row carries `ExtensionType`, `Label`,
31	`BpmnElement` (the host BPMN element), `ExtensionTag`, and
32	`RequiresDiscovery` (`Yes` means you must resolve a concrete resource — process,
33	queue, connection — before the node is runnable).
34	
35	## 2. Get the template for each chosen type
36	
37	```bash
38	uip maestro bpmn registry get <extensionType> --output json
39	```
40	
41	`Data.ExtensionType` contains everything needed to author the node:
42	
43	| Field | Use |
44	| --- | --- |
45	| `xmlTemplate` | The literal node XML with `{placeholder}` slots. **Author from this; fill placeholders only.** |
46	| `bpmnElement` | The host BPMN element the template uses (e.g. `bpmn:ServiceTask`). |
47	| `extensionTag` | `uipath:activity`, `uipath:event`, or `uipath:mapping`. |
48	| `contextFields[]` | The `uipath:context` inputs; each may carry its own `bindingInfo`. |
49	| `bindingInfo` | How the node binds to a resource (see §4). |
50	| `inputPattern` / `inputName` / `inputTarget` | How the request body input is shaped. |
51	| `requiresDiscovery` / `isDynamic` | Whether a concrete resource must be resolved first. |
52	
53	The placeholders you fill are the obvious ones: `{id}`, `{name}`,
54	`{incomingEdge}`, `{outgoingEdge}`, `{varId}` (the output variable id), plus the
55	per-context-field placeholders (`{releaseKey}`, `{queueName}`, `{appId}`, …) and
56	the body CDATA. Leave the structural placeholders (`{incomingEdge}` /
57	`{outgoingEdge}`) wired to the sequence-flow ids you create in
58	[structural-bpmn.md](structural-bpmn.md).
59	
60	## 3. Connector (`Intsvc.*`) enrichment
61	
62	For connector types (`requiresDiscovery: Yes`, e.g.
63	`Intsvc.ActivityExecution`, `Intsvc.WaitForEvent`, `Intsvc.EventTrigger`),
64	resolve a live connection and object, then enrich:
65	
66	```bash
67	uip is connections list --all-folders --output json   # pick a connection id + its connector (search all folders)
68	uip maestro bpmn registry get Intsvc.ActivityExecution \
69	    --connection-id <id> --object-name <object> --output json
70	```
71	
72	The response adds an `ISEnrichment` block with the live field metadata. Write
73	the activity's `body` input (`target="body"`) and `context` (`connectorKey`,
74	`objectName`) from that enrichment — do not hand-author connector schemas. The
75	connection is referenced through a connection binding, `=bindings.<bindingId>`
76	(see §4).
77	
78	## 4. Bindings — from `bindingInfo`, never invented
79	
80	A node that targets a cloud resource carries a binding. The `bindingInfo` on the
81	extension type tells you the binding shape; the concrete value comes from
82	discovery or the user.
83	
84	- **Resource bindings** (`bindingInfo.resource` = `process` / `queue` /
85	  `businessRule`): the context field named by `bindingInfo.contextField`
86	  (e.g. `releaseKey`, `queueName`) holds the resource key
87	  (`bindingInfo.propertyAttribute`, usually `Key`). Resolve the real key with
88	  `registry search` / discovered `Processes` / `Queues`; never guess a GUID.
89	- **Connection bindings** (`Intsvc.*`): the context references a connection via
90	  `=bindings.<bindingId>`, and a `<uipath:binding>` of `resource="Connection"`
91	  with `propertyAttribute="ConnectionId"` in the process-level
92	  `<uipath:bindings>` holds the live connection id from `uip is connections list`.
93	
94	Declare all bindings in a single process-level `<uipath:bindings version="v1">`
95	block. Each `<uipath:binding>` carries `id`, `resource`, `propertyAttribute`,
96	and a `default` value (the resolved key/id).
97	
98	## Agent wrapper selection — pick by `processType`, not the label
99	
100	When a node invokes an agent, choose the wrapper by the resource's
101	**`processType`** (from `uip or processes list --all-fields`), not its display
102	label:
103	
104	- Coded Python agents publish as `processType: "Function"` — use the
105	  `Orchestrator.StartJob` process contract, **not** `StartAgentJob`.
106	- Agent Builder (low-code) publishes as `processType: "Agent"` →
107	  `Orchestrator.StartAgentJob`.
108	- External A2A agent addressed by URL / skillId → `A2A.AgentExecution`.
109	- Integration Service external agent → `Intsvc.*AgentExecution`.
110	
111	Gotcha: `A2A.AgentExecution` renders as an external A2A node and **disables the
112	Action dropdown** in Studio Web. Do not use it for a folder-deployed agent — the
113	canvas treats the task as misconfigured. Use `StartAgentJob`/`StartJob` for
114	folder-deployed resources.
115	
116	## API workflow — wait vs fire-and-forget
117	
118	Pick the wrapper by whether downstream needs the invocation result:
119	`Orchestrator.ExecuteApiWorkflow` **waits** for completion (result available to
120	later nodes); `Orchestrator.ExecuteApiWorkflowAsync` **returns immediately**
121	(fire-and-forget). Both are `bpmn:serviceTask` activities. Resolve `ReleaseKey`
122	(process GUID), `FolderKey`/`FolderPath`, and the request/response schemas before
123	the node is runnable — make the wait-versus-async choice explicit in the model.
124	
125	## Integration Service triggers — bind trigger properties via the CLI
126	
127	`Intsvc.TimerTrigger` and `Intsvc.EventTrigger` (and connector waits like
128	`Intsvc.WaitForEvent`) need their **trigger properties** enriched/bound through
129	the CLI — the same enrichment path as `Intsvc.*` activities (§3). A hand-authored
130	trigger shell stays **draft** until the CLI supplies the concrete trigger
131	properties, connection binding, and schemas.
132	
133	## Connectionless vs connector HTTP
134	
135	- **Connector activity** (`Intsvc.ActivityExecution` / a connector-authenticated
136	  operation): use when the call goes through a tenant connection, a dynamic
137	  connector schema, or a connector object operation. Keep the node **draft**
138	  until enriched. The CLI-owned enrichment blockers — the ones that must be
139	  resolved before upload or run, and that boundary notes should name explicitly
140	  — are **connection binding**, **dynamic schemas**, generated **package
141	  metadata** (`bindings_v2.json`, `entry-points.json`, `operate.json`,
142	  `package-descriptor.json`). Do not hand-author any of these (§3).
143	- **Connectionless / manual HTTP** (`Intsvc.HttpExecution`, or
144	  `Intsvc.UnifiedHttpRequest` when current tooling exposes the unified shape):
145	  use when the workflow itself owns the URL, method, payload, and response
146	  parsing (no connection). Author `mode="manual"`, `method`, `url`, `headers`,
147	  `parameters`, `body` directly from the registry template.
148	
149	Status vocabulary for an IS node in a summary: **executable** (activity, inputs,
150	output variable, and downstream mappings present, runtime-verified if a run was
151	done), **draft** (BPMN shape/intent present but enrichment missing), **mock**
152	(returns fixed sample data instead of calling out), **blocked** (a required URL,
153	auth, schema, or enrichment decision is missing).
154	
155	## 5. Assemble
156	
157	1. Build the document scaffold and process (see
158	   [structural-bpmn.md](structural-bpmn.md)).
159	2. Declare root variables (`BPMN.Variables` template) and the
160	   `<uipath:bindings>` block.
161	3. For each node, paste its `registry get` `xmlTemplate`, fill placeholders, and
162	   wire `{incomingEdge}`/`{outgoingEdge}` to your sequence flows.
163	4. Author the structural BPMN the registry does not emit: sequence flows,
164	   gateway conditions/defaults, event definitions, boundary events,
165	   subprocess/call-activity containers, multi-instance markers.
166	5. Generate the `bpmndi:BPMNDiagram` (shape per node, edge per flow).
167	6. Validate (see [structural-bpmn.md#validation](structural-bpmn.md#validation)).
168	
169	## OOTB extension types (29, login-free)
170	
171	These are the built-in types `registry pull` returns without login. Discover the
172	exact template for any of them with `registry get <type>`.
173	
174	| Extension type | Host element | Tag |
175	| --- | --- | --- |
176	| `Actions.HITL` | `bpmn:UserTask` | activity |
177	| `Orchestrator.StartJob` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
178	| `Orchestrator.StartAgentJob` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
179	| `Orchestrator.BusinessRules` | `bpmn:BusinessRuleTask` | activity |
180	| `Orchestrator.ExecuteApiWorkflowAsync` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
181	| `Orchestrator.CreateQueueItem` | `bpmn:SendTask` | activity |
182	| `Orchestrator.CreateAndWaitForQueueItem` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
183	| `Orchestrator.StartAgenticProcess[Async]` | `bpmn:CallActivity` | activity |
184	| `Orchestrator.StartCaseMgmtProcess[Async]` | `bpmn:CallActivity` | activity |
185	| `Intsvc.ActivityExecution` | `bpmn:SendTask` | event/activity |
186	| `Intsvc.HttpExecution` / `Intsvc.UnifiedHttpRequest` | `bpmn:SendTask` | activity |
187	| `Intsvc.WaitForEvent` | `bpmn:ReceiveTask` | event |
188	| `Intsvc.EventTrigger` | `bpmn:StartEvent` | event |
189	| `Intsvc.TimerTrigger` | `bpmn:StartEvent` | activity |
190	| `Intsvc.{Async,SyncAgent,AsyncAgent,SyncWorkflow,AsyncWorkflow}Execution` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
191	| `A2A.AgentExecution` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
192	| `BPMN.Variables` | `bpmn:Task` | mapping |
193	| `BPMN.ScriptTask` | `bpmn:ScriptTask` | mapping |
194	| `Maestro.ReceiveMessageEvent` | `bpmn:IntermediateCatchEvent` | event |
195	| `Maestro.SendMessageEvent` | `bpmn:IntermediateThrowEvent` | event |
196	| `Maestro.CaseRulesEvaluator` / `Maestro.CaseManagerGuardrails` | `bpmn:ServiceTask` | activity |
197	
198	This table is a discovery aid, not a substitute for `registry get` — always pull
199	the live template before authoring.
200

parameters.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<bpmn:definitions
    xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL"
    xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI"
    xmlns:dc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC"
    xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI"
    xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    xmlns:uipath="http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn"
    id="Definitions_1" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn"
    exporter="UiPath (https://bpmn.uipath.com)" exporterVersion="1.0">

  <bpmn:process id="Process_1" isExecutable="true">
    <bpmn:extensionElements>
      <uipath:migrationVersion version="11.5" />
      <uipath:variables version="v1">
        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_InvoiceId" name="invoiceId" type="string" elementId="Start_Manual" direction="input" />
        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Amount" name="amount" type="number" elementId="Start_Manual" direction="input" />
        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Risk" name="risk" type="string" />
      </uipath:variables>
    </bpmn:extensionElements>

    <!-- Manual start event accepts invoiceId and amount -->
    <bpmn:startEvent id="Start_Manual" name="Start">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <uipath:event version="v1">
          <uipath:type value="BPMN.ManualStart" version="v1" />
          <uipath:entryPointId>Entry_ManualStart</uipath:entryPointId>
        </uipath:event>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_StartToClassify</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:startEvent>

    <!-- Script task: classifyInvoice sets risk variable -->
    <bpmn:scriptTask id="Task_ClassifyInvoice" name="classifyInvoice" scriptFormat="JavaScript">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <uipath:scriptVersion value="v3" />
        <uipath:mapping version="v1">
          <uipath:type value="BPMN.ScriptTask" version="v1" />
          <uipath:input name="args" type="json" target="bodyField"><![CDATA[{"amount":"=vars.Var_Amount"}]]></uipath:input>
          <uipath:output name="risk" type="string" var="Var_Risk" source="=result.response" />
        </uipath:mapping>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_StartToClassify</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_ClassifyToGateway</bpmn:outgoing>
      <bpmn:script><![CDATA[return { response: amount > 1000 ? "high" : "low" };]]></bpmn:script>
    </bpmn:scriptTask>

    <!-- Exclusive gateway: routeByRisk -->
    <bpmn:exclusiveGateway id="Gw_RouteByRisk" name="routeByRisk" default="Flow_LowRisk">
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_ClassifyToGateway</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_HighRisk</bpmn:outgoing>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_LowRisk</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:exclusiveGateway>

    <!-- User task: Review invoice (high-risk path) -->
    <bpmn:userTask id="Task_ReviewInvoice" name="Review invoice">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <uipath:activity version="v1">
          <uipath:type value="Actions.HITL" version="v1" />
          <uipath:context>
            <uipath:input name="appId" value="" />
            <uipath:input name="appVersion" value="" />
            <uipath:input name="actions" value="" />
            <uipath:input name="key" value="" />
            <uipath:input name="taskTitle" value="Review invoice" />
          </uipath:context>
          <uipath:input name="HitlTaskArguments" type="json" target="bodyField"><![CDATA[{"invoiceId":"=vars.Var_InvoiceId","amount":"=vars.Var_Amount","risk":"=vars.Var_Risk"}]]></uipath:input>
          <uipath:output name="Process response" type="Actions.HITL" var="" />
        </uipath:activity>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_HighRisk</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_ReviewToEnd</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:userTask>

    <!-- End event: auto-approved (low-risk default path) -->
    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_AutoApproved" name="auto-approved">
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_LowRisk</bpmn:incoming>
    </bpmn:endEvent>

    <!-- End event: reviewed (high-risk path after human review) -->
    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_Reviewed" name="reviewed">
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_ReviewToEnd</bpmn:incoming>
    </bpmn:endEvent>

    <!-- Sequence flows -->
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_StartToClassify" sourceRef="Start_Manual" targetRef="Task_ClassifyInvoice" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_ClassifyToGateway" sourceRef="Task_ClassifyInvoice" targetRef="Gw_RouteByRisk" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_HighRisk" sourceRef="Gw_RouteByRisk" targetRef="Task_ReviewInvoice">
      <bpmn:conditionExpression xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">=vars.Var_Risk == "high"</bpmn:conditionExpression>
    </bpmn:sequenceFlow>
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_LowRisk" sourceRef="Gw_RouteByRisk" targetRef="End_AutoApproved" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_ReviewToEnd" sourceRef="Task_ReviewInvoice" targetRef="End_Reviewed" />

<bpmn:definitions
    xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL"

    xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI"
    xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    xmlns:uipath="http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn"
    id="Definitions_1" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn"
    exporter="UiPath (https://bpmn.uipath.com)" exporterVersion="1.0">

  <bpmn:process id="Process_1" isExecutable="true">
    <bpmn:extensionElements>
      <uipath:migrationVersion version="11.5" />
      <uipath:variables version="v1">
        <uipath:input id="Var_InvoiceId" name="invoiceId" type="string" elementId="Start_Manual" />
        <uipath:input id="Var_Amount" name="amount" type="number" elementId="Start_Manual" />
        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Risk" name="risk" type="string" />
      </uipath:variables>
    </bpmn:extensionElements>

    <!-- Manual start event: accepts invoiceId and amount variables -->
    <bpmn:startEvent id="Start_Manual" name="Start">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <uipath:entryPointId value="Entry_ManualStart" />
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_StartToClassify</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:startEvent>

    <!-- Script task: classifyInvoice sets risk variable -->
    <bpmn:scriptTask id="Task_ClassifyInvoice" name="classifyInvoice" scriptFormat="JavaScript">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <uipath:scriptVersion value="v3" />
        <uipath:mapping version="v1">
          <uipath:type value="BPMN.ScriptTask" version="v1" />
          <uipath:input name="args" type="json" target="bodyField"><![CDATA[{"amount":"=vars.Var_Amount"}]]></uipath:input>
          <uipath:output name="risk" type="string" var="Var_Risk" source="=result.response" />
        </uipath:mapping>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_StartToClassify</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_ClassifyToGateway</bpmn:outgoing>
      <bpmn:script><![CDATA[return { response: amount > 1000 ? "high" : "low" };]]></bpmn:script>
    </bpmn:scriptTask>

    <!-- Exclusive gateway: routeByRisk; default path goes to auto-approved -->
    <bpmn:exclusiveGateway id="Gw_RouteByRisk" name="routeByRisk" default="Flow_LowRisk">
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_ClassifyToGateway</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_HighRisk</bpmn:outgoing>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_LowRisk</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:exclusiveGateway>

    <bpmn:userTask id="Task_ReviewInvoice" name="Review invoice">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>

          <uipath:context>

            <uipath:input name="appVersion" value="" />

          <uipath:output name="Process response" type="Actions.HITL" var="" />
        </uipath:activity>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_HighRisk</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_ReviewToEnd</bpmn:outgoing>

      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_LowRisk</bpmn:incoming>

    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_Reviewed" name="reviewed">

    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_ReviewToEnd" sourceRef="Task_ReviewInvoice" targetRef="End_Reviewed" />
  </bpmn:process>
